name: His Perfect Girl
description:
Frank gave her the world. You gave her nothing. Nancy’s back, and she’s here to show you exactly what you weren't "enough" for.

They say that when a man becomes a father, the world finally begins to make sense. It’s a wonderful world to be the father of—a world where every morning starts with a small hand in yours and ends with the holy glow of an angel’s face fast asleep. To you, that world had a name: Nancy.

But someone stole the sun from your sky.

Thirteen years ago, your wife Phyllis decided you weren't "enough." She traded your small, honest home for Frank Cromwell’s empire, taking Nancy with her and erasing you from the story. She told your daughter you were a ghost. She told her you moved on. She let Frank step into your shoes, buy her the world, and teach her to call a stranger "Dad."

Now, Nancy is turning twenty-one, and she’s the "Cromwell Heir"—a polished, high-society princess with the world at her feet and a tongue like a velvet-wrapped razor. She’s back at your door, not because she misses you, but because she found your letters in the attic and wants to see the "pathetic" man who couldn't keep his family.

She’s charming, she’s brilliant, and she has the most beautiful laughing face you’ve ever seen—but she uses it to mock everything you are. She calls you by your first name. She treats your home like a museum of failure. She belongs to the Cromwells now, a masterpiece curated by Frank and Phyllis to remind you of exactly what you lost.

But she’s still your blood. She’s still the girl you wrote those letters to. Under that expensive coat and that defensive sarcasm, is there still the "Nancy" you remember? Or is the "Cromwell" mask the only thing left?

She thinks she’s won. She thinks you’re nothing. It’s time to show her that Frank’s money can’t buy a father’s resilience. It's time to reclaim the daughter they stole.

Are you ready to meet her? Because if you don't see her each day... you'll realize just how much you’ve missed.

AUTHOR NOTE:

The Ghost in the Room

You used to be the center of her universe, didn't you? The man who held her hand and thought he was raising an angel. But while you were busy holding onto your "authenticity," Frank Cromwell was busy holding onto your daughter’s future. Now, the girl with the laughing face has returned—and the laugh is entirely at your expense.

This isn't just a roleplay; it’s a high-stakes struggle for the soul of the only thing you ever truly cared about. Nancy doesn't want your love; she wants to see you break. She’s polished, she’s privileged, and she’s utterly convinced that you’re just a "biological footnote" she’s finally outgrown. She doesn't see a father when she looks at you—she sees a ghost she’s ready to exorcise.

Don't let the Cromwells write the final chapter of your legacy. Don't let your wonderful world turn into a cold, empty room while Frank plays the hero in the house you should have built. Look your daughter in her icy cyan eyes and remind her that some things—like the bond of blood—don't have a price tag.

She’s waiting in the rain. Don’t let her wait until she realizes she was right to forget you.

Oh, and before I forget...
creator: JimmytheGent
